## APRENDIZADOS-CHAVE

1. **Email should drive 25-30% of total ecommerce revenue** — this is the benchmark across 2-dozen clients at Boundless Labs. ^[HEUR-CD-001]

2. **Welcome emails generate 320% more revenue per email than regular promos** — the welcome series is the highest-converting flow with ~3% conversion rate. ^[HEUR-CD-005, HEUR-CD-004]

3. **Three abandonment levels require different treatment** — Browse (1% conversion, low intent), Cart (medium intent), Checkout (high intent). Always prioritize deeper funnel actions. ^[MM-CD-003, FIL-CD-018]

4. **A single abandoned cart flow added $4,000+/month** for one client — quick win with massive revenue impact from a single automation. ^[HEUR-CD-003]

5. **VIP segmentation nearly doubles conversion rates** — VIP abandoned checkout: 11.6% vs regular: 5.8%. Same flow, different messaging, 2x results. ^[MM-CD-016]

6. **Back-in-stock notifications have 31-34% placed order rates** — highest intent email in the entire stack, serves dual purpose of list building and revenue. ^[MM-CD-018]

7. **Smart sending (16-24 hour window) prevents fatigue** — prevents overlap where someone receives both a campaign and a flow email on the same day. ^[MM-CD-005]

8. **Browse abandonment drives $3K-$5K/month for 7-8 figure clients** — triggered emails with 63% higher open rates than campaigns. First email at 2-hour delay is optimal. ^[HEUR-CD-010, HEUR-CD-008, HEUR-CD-012]

9. **Secret surveys through tracking links** — each link click tags subscriber profiles with interests, enabling hyper-targeted content without traditional surveys. ^[MM-CD-015]

10. **Transactional emails are marketing opportunities** — order confirmation, shipping, and delivery emails get extremely high open rates. Use them for cross-sells and referrals. ^[MM-CD-012]

---

## PADROES DECISORIOS

### Flow prioritization hierarchy
Priority by funnel depth (highest first):
1. Abandoned Checkout (highest intent)
2. Abandoned Cart (medium intent)
3. Browse Abandonment (lowest intent)
4. Welcome Series (new subscribers)
5. Post-Purchase / Thank You
6. Review Request -> Cross-Sell / Upsell
7. Customer Winback / Replenishment
8. Breakup / Sunset (list hygiene)
^[FIL-CD-018, MM-CD-002]

### When to offer discounts
- **Abandoned cart/checkout:** Discount codes significantly boost conversion. Test 10-15% off. ^[FIL-CD-014]
- **Welcome series:** Include initial discount to drive first purchase. ^[HEUR-CD-006]
- **Exit intent popup:** Offer higher discount than normal — these visitors would have generated zero revenue otherwise. ^[MM-CD-017]
- **Breakup series:** Last-chance discount before removing from list. ^[FIL-CD-013]

### Timing decisions
- First welcome email: within 10 minutes ^[HEUR-CD-007]
- Browse abandonment first email: 2 hours after browse ^[HEUR-CD-012]
- Browse abandonment series: 2-3 emails over 2-3 days ^[HEUR-CD-011]
- Smart sending window: 16-24 hours between emails ^[MM-CD-005]
- Winback/replenishment: match product lifecycle (30-day supply = trigger at day 25-30) ^[MM-CD-013]

### When to clean the list
- Unengaged subscribers hurt deliverability and cost money ^[FIL-CD-013]
- ISPs track engagement signals — negative signals push to spam ^[MM-CD-014]
- Run sunset/breakup flows to re-engage or remove ^[FIL-CD-013]
